input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern `.runsettings` format after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their files to understand what they have, and then use the appropriate skill to help with the migration.

Let me first look at their files, and since this is about MSTest migration, the `migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3` skill seems relevant as it mentions "replacing .testsettings with .runsettings".

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** in modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (30 seconds) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout; use [Timeout] attribute for per-test control -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- DeploymentEnabled replaces the old deployment/assembly-resolution behavior -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
I've created `local.runsettings` as the replacement. Here's what changed:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` (not needed in SDK-style projects) |
| Code coverage data collector (v1.0 URI) | Updated to v2.0 URI under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

**To use it**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add to your `.csproj`:
  ```xml
  <PropertyGroup>
    <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
  </PropertyGroup>
  ```

You can now delete the old `local.testsettings` file — MSTest v3 will silently ignore it.
